decision: Extract shared _parse_sections(content)->list[{title,body}] reused by both plain-text split and embed builder; [] when no ## section
reasoning: one ## boundary rule prevents drift; [] is the single explicit fallback trigger

decision: _repo_color = int(hashlib.md5(slug)) % len(6-color palette); never builtin hash()
reasoning: builtin hash() is PYTHONHASHSEED-salted per process -> breaks color idempotency; md5 is a stable non-security integer digest

decision: _build_embeds maps each section->embed{title,description<=4096,color,footer='OSS Pulse • {ts}'}; over-length body line-split via reused _split_lines(body,4096); len(str)=code points
reasoning: reuse proven two-level splitter; drop no content (vs truncate); code-point not byte measure

decision: _batch_embeds chunks into <=10-embed batches, POST each sequentially via a payload-parameterized _post_one (content vs embeds body)
reasoning: reuse single timeout + URL-never-leaked error path; fatal-at-k, no rollback identical to plain text; parameterize one helper not fork

decision: deliver(): if use_embeds and _parse_sections()!=[] -> build+batch+POST embeds, else plain-text _split_for_discord path; use_embeds=false byte-identical to pre-change
reasoning: structural []-trigger fallback means embed formatting alone never fails a run; signature unchanged

decision: _validate_discord_use_embeds(data): type(v) is not bool -> ConfigError; default False; wire into load_config + Config(discord_use_embeds=)
reasoning: mirrors existing _validate_delta/_validate_etag_cache bool-trap; fail-fast at load
rejected: isinstance check (accepts bool subclass of int)
